evidence_snippets.text: You can apply for a travel grant during your application if your travel costs are not reimbursed by your employer.
Our jury will select participants and approve the travel grant within availability.
If your travel grant was approved, you need to hand in receipts for all expenses that occurred in connection with your travels after completing the Summer School.

Upcoming Summer SChool CISPA ZINE 7 : THE CISPA SUMMER SCHOOL The seventh issue of our Zine takes you inside the Summer School experience - where young talents, researchers, and industry experts come together to learn, collaborate, and explore the future of cybersecurity and AI.
If you attended one of our Summer Schools in the past, you are of course welcome to apply again for any of the upcoming Summer Schools.
It is advisable to mention a specific research focus in your application that aligns with your interests and your prior education.
Due to the high number of applications we receive, our jury has strict criteria for selecting participants for our Summer Schools.
criteria for selecting participants for our Summer Schools.
A foundational knowledge in the field is required, and participants should have reached a certain academic level.
How does the travel grant work?
The participation fee secures your spot for the event and covers catering, drinks, course materials and the entire program.
It cannot be refunded via the travel grant.
Only receipts dated within one day before the start of the event until the last day are eligible for reimbursement.
ools are aimed at students and PhD students in relevant areas of cybersecurity.
The event typically features talks, workshops, discussions, poster sessions by participants as well as a social program.
If there are two Summer Schools in one year, you can apply for both of these Summer Schools.
What criteria are considered in the selection process of participants?
A foundational knowledge in the field is required, and participants should
We give priority to those who either have a background in the Summer School’s topic or are currently studying it.
Please book accommodations from our recommendation list if possible or contact us for approval prior to your booking.

Participants The SOLAS Summer School is open to an international cohort of about 60 early career researchers.
We are committed to assembling a truly diverse and inclusive cohort, welcoming participants from various geographic regions, backgrounds, abilities and identities.

Objectives The SOLAS Summer School aims to provide a holistic understanding of ocean-atmosphere sciences, equipping participants with the transdisciplinary skills needed to address pressing climate and environmental challenges.
The programme is primarily designed for PhD students and early-stage postdoctoral researchers, but open for applicants with different career paths.
This is an Ocean Decade Endorsed Activity General Information Read more Application and Registration Fees The call for applications is closed.
Read more Contact and Organisers The Summer School 2026 is organised by an international team of researchers and the IPO.

Participants will explore the complex interactions between the ocean, atmosphere, and climate through a dynamic blend of lectures, hands-on practicals, collaborative student-led projects, all aligned with the "SOLAS Science Towards Solutions" SOLAS 3.0 framework.
Our goal is to empower you to translate fundamental knowledge into solutions across the science-policy nexus and thrive in a collaborative, international and diverse research environment.
Student backgrounds will represent the full breadth of SOLAS sciences, from fundamental ocean-atmosphere research to applied solution oriented research with direct societal relevance.
Read more Contact Please send an email to solasschool@xmu.edu.cn if you have any questions or need further assistance regarding the Summer School.
The SOLAS international research network is built on interdisciplinary synergy and a deep commitment to fostering the next generation of scientists.
Building on a legacy of training over 600 early career scientists since 2003, the SOLAS Summer School provides a unique opportunity to learn from world-leading experts in ocean-atmosphere sciences.

items.page_title: OPLSS 2026 | University of Oregon

evidence_snippets.text: ﻿ OPLSS 2026 | University of Oregon One Stop Apply Visit Give June 22-July 4, 2026 Types, Proofs, and Program Logics Topics Schedule Participants Logistics Archives Policies Sponsors For 24 years, the Oregon Programming Languages Summer School (OPLSS) has focused on teaching the principles of programming languages to students and professionals.

Although the topics change from year to year, a common theme is the significance of fundamental theory in the design and implementation of programming languages, the development of program verification tools, and the application of advanced programming languages.
The summer school attracts participants from around the world and often subsidizes the participation of qualified attendees with limited resources.
Since its inception in 2002, over a thousand participants have attended OPLSS.
The program lasts two weeks and allows ample time for both group and independent study, as well as opportunities to enjoy the many recreational activities available around Eugene.
Recordings of the sessions are available online.

items.existing_candidate_title: 2026 Technical Short Course: InSAR Processing and Theory using GMTSAR

evidence_snippets.text: July 20-22, and August 7 Location: Virtual Deadline: June 12, 2026 APPLICATIONS ARE NOW CLOSED Course Description: This course covers the theory and application of repeat-pass synthetic aperture radar interferometry (InSAR) using the GMTSAR software package.

Participants will learn foundational SAR/InSAR concepts and work hands-on to generate interferograms from test datasets (including recently available NISAR data) and (optionally) construct deformation time series.
Participants attend plenary sessions and may work with instructors in small groups to learn GMTSAR and prepare an oral presentation.
Secondary Audience: Participants who plan to attend plenary sessions only and may already have some InSAR processing experience, and are seeking updated theory and methods from the instructor team.
Participant Commitment: Participants attend 3 online sessions (4 hours each).
Primary Audience: Graduate and undergraduate students in Earth Science; early-career geoscientists in university, government, or industry.
